references.text: [Er37b] Erd\H{o}s, Paul, On the Sum and {D}ifference of Squares of {P}rimes. J. London Math. Soc. (1937), 133--136.

remarks.text: Erd\H{o}s \cite{Er37b} proved this is true when $k=2$, and also when $k=3$ (but this proof appears to be unpublished).

statement.text: Let $k\geq 2$, and let $f_k(n)$ count the number of solutions to\[n=p_1^k+\cdots+p_k^k,\]where the $p_i$ are prime numbers. Is it true that $\limsup f_k(n)=\infty$?
